feat: build animation — tiles rise from underground on room paint

When a paint stroke is committed the newly placed tiles cascade up from
below ground in a diagonal wave. The animation starts at the release
corner (opposite diagonal from where the drag started) and sweeps toward
the stroke origin, so tiles near the release appear first.

Architecture
- src/store/buildAnimations.ts: module-level registry, no React state,
  triggerBuild(cells, originCell) + getBuildYOffset(key, now, extraDelay)
- Grid.tsx: calls triggerBuild(newCells, strokeCurrentCell) after paintCells
- DungeonRoom.tsx: AnimatedTileGroup wrapper uses useFrame to set Y offset
  each frame; walls trail their floor tile by 70 ms

Constants: BUILD_DEPTH=3 wu, RISE_DURATION_MS=450, MAX_STAGGER_MS=320,
cubic ease-out curve.

commit.message:
nfwd: canonical slot traversal via `_fold_slots` / `_unfold_slots` (#1136)

* Refactor nfwd: canonical slot traversal via _fold_slots / _unfold_slots

Add two traversal helpers to the Nfwd module that define the canonical
slot order exactly once:

- _fold_slots(f, init, x, state): left fold over scalar slots
  (real=1, complex=2 re/im, arrays in eachindex, tuples L→R)
- _unfold_slots(f, x, state): leaf-level structural map over
  primitive leaves (scalars, complex, arrays; tuples recurse)

Refactor existing nfwd machinery to use these helpers:

- _nfwd_input_dof: now a one-liner fold that counts slots
- _nfwd_seed_tangents: tuple recursion replaced by _unfold_slots
- _nfwd_scatter_scalar_chunk: replaces both itself and the recursive
  _nfwd_update_scalar_grad with a single _unfold_slots traversal

Add focused tests verifying the traversal contract: real/complex
scalars, dense real/complex arrays, tuple mixtures, Float32, and
explicit checks that fold and unfold visit slots in the same order.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix nfwd scrutinise findings: docstrings, DOF counting, comment

- Rewrite _fold_slots / _unfold_slots docstrings: each slot is one
  differentiable scalar DOF; complex contributes real-then-imaginary;
  tuples left to right; arrays in eachindex order
- Restore O(1) _nfwd_input_dof specialisations for IEEEFloat, Complex,
  and AbstractArray (fold-based counting was O(n) for arrays)
- Correct section comment: CUDA extension uses _nfwd_leaf_dof /
  _nfwd_slot_meta, not NfwdMooncake

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Simplify: collapse _unfold_slots leaf overloads; fix fragile test

- Collapse four identical _unfold_slots leaf overloads (IEEEFloat,
  Complex, AbstractArray real/complex) into a single Union dispatch;
  same allowlist semantics, no code duplication
- Replace sum_slots test helper with sum_slot_idx: the old helper
  reimplemented _nfwd_add_slot! slot-index conventions (cld, isodd)
  in test code; new helper sums within-leaf slot indices instead,
  testing the same callback contract without the fragile coupling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Drop _nfwd_leaf_dof_type / _nfwd_leaf_dof / _nfwd_slot_meta

These were shims for MooncakeCUDAExt; the CUDA extension will be
refactored to use _fold_slots / _unfold_slots directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor CUDA ext to use _nfwd_input_dof instead of removed helpers

Replace _nfwd_leaf_dof_type, _nfwd_leaf_dof, and _nfwd_slot_meta usage in
MooncakeCUDAExt with _nfwd_type_dof and _nfwd_input_dof via a thin
_gpu_rep_element dispatch that maps broadcast leaf args to representative
scalar elements.

Fix NegotiationMatcherPolicy invalidating higher-priority endpoints (#65973)

* Fix NegotiationMatcherPolicy invalidating higher-priority endpoints

NegotiationMatcherPolicy.ApplyAsync has two invalidation points that do
not account for routing priority (CandidateState.Score). When a
lower-priority endpoint (e.g. a catch-all with ContentEncodingMetadata)
matches the Accept-Encoding header, it blindly invalidates all preceding
candidates — including higher-priority endpoints representing different
resources.

This manifests when MapStaticAssets registers catch-all endpoints (e.g.
{**path:nonfile} for SPA fallback) with gzip/br/identity variants. A
request to a specific API or configuration endpoint (e.g.
_blazor/_configuration) that shares a DFA path node with these catch-alls
gets incorrectly overridden by the catch-all's gzip variant.

The fix leverages the fact that candidates are already sorted by
ascending Score. A scoreTierStart variable tracks where the current
score tier begins. Backward sweeps in EvaluateCandidate use
Math.Max(bestMatchIndex, scoreTierStart) as the start bound, and
post-match invalidation skips candidates with strictly lower score
than the best match. This preserves higher-priority endpoints without
adding per-element Score comparisons.

* Add integration tests for ContentEncodingNegotiationMatcherPolicy

End-to-end tests that exercise the full DFA routing pipeline with real
RouteEndpoint instances and route patterns. Tests run through both the
INodeBuilderPolicy path (DFA branch construction) and the
IEndpointSelectorPolicy path (runtime candidate filtering).

Key scenarios:
- Basic encoding negotiation (gzip/br/identity selection)
- Literal endpoint preserved when catch-all has encoding metadata
- Multiple catch-all encoding variants don't defeat literal endpoints
- Parameterized endpoints preserved over catch-alls with metadata
- Mixed priority tiers (literal > parameterized > catch-all)
- Static file encoding variants with catch-all present
- 406 when no encoding matches and no identity fallback

* Fix scoreTierStart tracking and add mixed quality test

- Use separate currentTierScore variable instead of candidates[scoreTierStart].Score
  to avoid reading bitwise-negated scores after SetValidity invalidates candidates
- Add Match_ContentEncoding_AcceptQualityWins_WhenBothAcceptAndEndpointQualityDiffer test

fix: repair redirect tester + add GET /api/links/{link_id} endpoint (#19)

## Summary

Fixes the **Redirect tester** feature in the UI, which was silently
broken due to a browser security restriction.

## Root cause

The frontend `testRedirect()` function used `fetch()` with `redirect:
'manual'`. For cross-origin requests (frontend on `:5173`, backend on
`:8000`) this produces an **opaque redirect** response — the browser
hides all headers including `Location`, and `res.status` is always `0`.
As a result, the tester always showed blank status and location.

## Changes

### Backend
- Added `GET /api/links/{link_id}` endpoint that returns `LinkOut`
metadata without triggering a redirect — a clean REST resource read

### Frontend
- `testRedirect()` now calls `GET /api/links/{link_id}` and displays
**redirect code**, **target URL**, and **active** status
- Added `getLink()` helper to `api.ts`

### Tests
- Two new integration tests for the new endpoint (200 with metadata, 404
for unknown ID)
- All 30 tests pass, linting clean

## Also on this branch
- `docs: remove rate limiting requirement` — minor docs update

* fix: skip cache.saveCache() on Windows to work around @actions/glob bug (actions/toolkit#2085)

* fix: provide require polyfill in ESM bundle so minimatch resolves path.sep correctly on Windows

The root cause of the Windows cache bug (actions/toolkit#2085) is that
minimatch v3 uses `require('path')` with a try/catch fallback to
`{ sep: '/' }`. When bundled as ESM by rollup, `require` is not
available, so the fallback activates and `path.sep` becomes '/' on
Windows. This disables minimatch's built-in backslash-to-forward-slash
conversion in its match() method, causing @actions/glob to never match
any paths.

The fix adds `createRequire` from Node's `module` builtin as a rollup
banner/intro, making `require` available in the ESM bundle. This allows
minimatch's `require('path')` to resolve correctly, enabling proper
Windows path handling without patching any dependencies.

Also reverts the "skip saveCache on Windows" workaround so caching
works on all platforms.
